channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Dennis Walder Explores His Mother’s Past in South African Memoir
item.description: Dennis Walder, literary scholar wey born for South Africa, don publish him memoir called 'Amid the Alien Corn: A Son’s Memoir'. For the book, e dey trace how e notice as small pikin say him mama, Ruth...

item.title: Amit Shah criticises Youth Congress shirtless protest at Delhi AI Summit
item.description: Union Home Minister Amit Shah, speaking at a rally in Guwahati, sharply criticised the Congress party for a shirtless protest by its youth wing at the AI Impact Summit held in New Delhi last month. Sh...

item.title: Danish King and Queen Begin First Official Visit to Australia with Uluru Trek
item.description: Denmark's King Frederik and Queen Mary have kicked off their first official visit to Australia since being crowned in 2024 with a sunrise walk to the culturally significant Muṯitjulu Waterhole at Ulur...

item.title: Bobby McMann Debuts with Two Goals as Seattle Kraken Beat Vancouver Canucks 5-2
item.description: In a strong showing on Saturday, the Seattle Kraken secured a 5-2 victory over the Vancouver Canucks, powered by newcomer Bobby McMann. Playing his first game after being acquired from the Toronto Map...

item.title: Experiencing the Grateful Dead: A Transformative Musical Journey
item.description: Deroy Murdock recounts his first experience attending a Grateful Dead concert in 1987, describing it as an unexpectedly profound experience. Initially skeptical, Murdock joined his friends at the Irvi...

item.title: Analysis of Recent Israel-Iran Conflict Escalation
item.description: For the past two weeks, the world has watched a dangerous escalation in the Middle East. What started as decades of suspicion, proxy battles, nuclear fears, and political mistrust has now turned into ...

item.title: Kaizer Chiefs Executive Challenges 15-Year Debt Dispute Over R4.3 Million
item.description: Bobby Motaung, the football manager of Kaizer Chiefs, is contesting a legal move to seize his shares in the club over an alleged debt of R4.3 million. The debt dates back to more than ten years ago an...

item.title: Labour Party Focuses on Strengthening Grassroots Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: The Labour Party don yan say dem no dey plan to boycott the 2027 general elections. Nenadi Usman, wey be Interim National Chairman of the party, talk this one during the launch of the party's e-regist...

item.title: Nollywood Actor Femi Adewole Clears Air On Rumours About Aunty Ajara
item.description: Nollywood actor Femi Adewole, wey dem also sabi as Femi Apefe, don talk on top recent claims wey dey link am to the death of actress Aunty Ajara. According to reports, the actress pass away due to com...

item.title: Eye of the Match Lets You Play as a Football Referee in a Puzzle-Like Simulation
item.description: Eye of the Match is an innovative video game that transforms the traditional football experience by putting players in the role of a virtual referee. Inspired by games like Papers, Please, the title e...

item.title: Weekly Highlights from Moss & Fog: Nostalgia, Design, and Culture
item.description: The latest edition of Moss & Fog's Fog Signals, covering March 8–14, 2026, offers a curated look at unique and visually striking stories from around the world. This week’s top posts include a mesmeriz...

item.title: Canberra Trainer Enters Music Time in Golden Slipper with $150,000 Late Fee
item.description: Canberra trainer Gratz Vella has taken a bold step by entering his two-year-old horse, Music Time, into the Golden Slipper at Rosehill, paying a $150,000 late entry fee. The Golden Slipper offers a fi...

item.title: Kerala Assembly Election 2026: Voting on April 9, Counting on May 4
item.description: The Election Commission of India has officially announced the schedule for the 2026 Kerala Assembly elections. Polling for all 140 seats will be conducted in a single phase on April 9, with the counti...

item.title: Trump Urges International Support to Secure Strait of Hormuz Amid Tensions With Iran
item.description: US President Donald Trump don call on other countries make dem join hand with America to keep Strait of Hormuz open. Na Saturday, for his Truth Social post, Trump talk say plenty countries wey dey aff...

item.title: Government launches export hub and youth training to boost industrial growth and Vision 2050
item.description: The Tanzanian government has recently launched a Sourcing and Export Centre along with a Youth Investor Training Programme, aiming to transform the strategic goals of Vision 2050 into real outcomes. T...

item.title: Republic of Congo Holds Presidential Election Amid Sassou Nguesso's Bid for Continued Leadership
item.description: The Republic of Congo went to the polls on Sunday, March 15, 2026, in a presidential election widely seen as extending President Denis Sassou Nguesso's nearly 42-year grip on power. At 82 years old, S...

item.title: Arena Sports Show Discusses Pirates and Sundowns Neck-and-Neck in Premiership
item.description: In the 95th episode of the Arena Sports Show, Clauiee Grace Mpanza, joined by Mahlatse Mphahlele and Sizwe Mabena, delved into the latest developments in South African football. The main focus was the...

item.title: Sarfraz Ahmed Announces Retirement After Two-Decade International Career
item.description: LAHORE: Sarfraz Ahmed, former Pakistan cricket captain, officially retired from international cricket on Sunday, ending a distinguished career that spanned nearly twenty years. In his statement releas...

item.title: Philippine Official Denies China's Claim of Scarborough Shoal Concession
item.description: A senior official of the Philippine Coast Guard, Rear Admiral Jay Tarriela, clarified on Sunday that a letter allegedly written in 1990 by former Philippine ambassador Bienvenido A. Tan Jr. does not p...

item.title: Punjab Government to File Defamation Cases Over Misuse Allegations of Official Aircraft
item.description: The Punjab government has announced that it will take legal action against those spreading false information regarding the use of the provincial government's official aircraft. Senior Minister Marriyu...
